_comment: Address plan for gen_clab_topology.py. Every address is POSITION-ENCODED so you can read role/plane/index straight out of it. Edit the bases/host-bits freely; the encoding layout is documented per section.

hostname_prefixes._comment: names are 1-based. single plane: spine1/leaf1/gpu1. multi-plane switches: spine1-p1 (prefix + index + '-' + plane_tag + plane). GPUs never carry a plane tag (one GPU spans all planes).

role_ids._comment: single hex nibble per role; used as the high nibble of the position hextet RPII = role(1) plane(1) index(2).

mgmt._comment: static mgmt IPv4 = subnet.network + role_base[role] + (plane-1)*stride[role] + index. /22 = 172.20.20.0-172.20.23.255 (~1022 usable), holds hundreds of nodes. Blocks: spine .20.11-, leaf .20.101-, gpu starts at network+256 = .21.1 (so gpu index 1..256 -> .21.1-.22.0, no collision with leaf/spine, no broadcast). The generator HARD-FAILS on any collision, on the .1 gateway, or on out-of-subnet — widen the subnet further or raise gpu role_base to scale past ~256 GPUs.
mgmt._was: 172.20.20.0/24 with gpu role_base 180 — only fit ~74 GPUs before hitting .255.

loopback._comment: switch loopback = {base}:{RPII}::{host}/128 where RPII hextet = role_id*0x1000 + plane*0x100 + index. e.g. spine2 in plane3 -> fd00:0:0:1302::1. base must be <= 3 hextets.

p2p._comment: leaf<->spine link = {base}:{plane}:{LLSS}::/127 where LLSS = leaf*0x100 + spine. leaf end = ::{leaf_host}, spine end = ::{spine_host}. e.g. plane3 leaf1<->spine2 -> fd00:1:3:102::/127 (leaf .. ::0, spine .. ::1). base must be <= 2 hextets.

gpu._comment: every GPU has a NIC into every leaf. each gpu-NIC<->leaf subnet = {base}:{plane}:{LLGG}::/64 where LLGG = leaf*0x100 + gpu. gpu side = ::{gpu_host}, leaf gw = ::{leaf_host}. e.g. plane2 leaf1 gpu3 -> fd00:9:2:103::/64 (gpu ::2, leaf gw ::1). decodes to plane+leaf+gpu. base must be <= 2 hextets.

srv6._comment: per-switch SRv6 locator = {block}:{RPII}::/48 (same RPII encoding as loopback). End/uN = the locator. End.DT6 (decap, on GPU leaves) = {locator}:{func_end_dt6}::. End.X toward neighbor n = {locator}:{func_endx_base + n}::. block must be <= 2 hextets so block:RPII = /48.

srv6_spec._comment: MRC-spec NIC-decap mode (--spec-mode). 32-bit block fcbb:bb00 (F3216, matches the paper/Cisco). Switch locator = {block}:{RPII}::/48, behaviour uN (classic), End.X per neighbor (func_endx_base) for the up/down hops. Decap moves to the NIC: each GPU gets a decap uSID = gpu_usid_base + global_gpu_index, decap SID = {block}:{gpu_usid}::, programmed End.DT6 on the GPU. The dst leaf holds a PLAIN /48 forward route {block}:{gpu_usid}::/48 -> GPU port (no SID action — keeps the core dumb). Carrier = {block}:{T1 uSID}:{T0 uSID}:{gpu_usid}::, inner DA = dst GPU real addr. Leaves carry NO End.DT6 in this mode (func_end_dt6 kept only for the GPU's own decap SID).
